title: fagara guineensis
intro: Fagara guineensis, also known as Guinea pepper or African long pepper, is a plant species belonging to the Rutaceae family, native to tropical Africa. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Fagara guineensis include relief from pain and fever, as well as treatment for respiratory and digestive issues. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions, decoctions, or as a spice, and is also used to make a medicinal oil that can be applied topically to treat skin conditions.
